snippets.pull_quote: It felt like the Wall in Game of Thrones — trying to scale that when you're not starting.
snippets.website_angle: Speak to operators who feel the pipeline is impossible to crack from a standing start.

snippets.pull_quote: It's very easy when there's a load to do to put off the things that are creating new business. 30 to 40% of my job needs to be trying to win new clients.
snippets.website_angle: Name the trap: delivery work crowds out business development every time.

snippets.pull_quote: I'm constantly worried I'm not doing enough — not letting my clients down, but I'm letting myself down.
snippets.website_angle: Surface the guilt operators feel about neglecting their own growth, not just client work.

snippets.pull_quote: I'm just telling myself I'll do it and then not doing it. Outbound is what I really don't know how to do — that's my next thing.
snippets.website_angle: Position the product as the thing that finally makes outbound feel doable, not dreadable.

snippets.pull_quote: Without guidance and shape to the week, you can end up putting in a lot of effort without any momentum.
snippets.website_angle: Contrast busy-ness with actual progress — the product gives direction, not just a to-do list.

snippets.pull_quote: Overwhelm and decision paralysis about what to do next — I just need something that tells me: do this next.
snippets.website_angle: Lead with 'always know your next move' — remove the cognitive load of prioritisation.

snippets.pull_quote: Selling yourself is very, very different from working in an agency and selling someone else — especially when they've got a 10 million pound budget.
snippets.website_angle: Validate the identity shift; great operators aren't natural self-promoters.

snippets.pull_quote: How do I position myself so I'm not limiting it, but I'm not too general? I spent a long time on that — when it clicked, it was good.
snippets.website_angle: Acknowledge the niche-vs-generalist tension as a real, painful early-stage decision.

snippets.pull_quote: They're constantly tinkering — with their website, their positioning, their framework — rather than just going out and doing the selling.
snippets.website_angle: Call out productive procrastination; the product redirects energy toward revenue actions.

snippets.pull_quote: I had to go to Claude and ask — can I do go-to-market? And it's like, of course I can fucking do it. I've been doing it for years. But the confidence just isn't there.
snippets.website_angle: Speak to the imposter syndrome that hits even seasoned operators when they go independent.

snippets.pull_quote: I've got a runway of money for how long it will last — and that's always in the background.
snippets.website_angle: Acknowledge the financial clock ticking; urgency is real, not imagined.

snippets.pull_quote: He's great, but he's throwing stuff at me — and it's how I set boundaries to say I'm only fractional. That's more about me. It's my transition from corporate.
snippets.website_angle: Help operators protect their time even with clients they love working with.
